title: Gentle Comforting Irish Beef Stew
description: A comforting and briny Irish dish featuring tender beef in a rich and silky beef stock, cooked to perfection in a bain-marie, and suitable for a macrobiotic diet. This hearty stew is made with wholesome ingredients and gentle cooking methods.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 300 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large Dutch oven, heat 2 tablespoons of oil over medium heat.
Add the chopped onions and cook until they are translucent and starting to caramelize.
Add the chopped celery and cook for an additional 2 minutes.
Add the beef cubes and cook until they are browned on all sides.
Add the chopped carrots and potatoes, beef stock, sea salt, and black pepper, and stir to combine.
Cover the Dutch oven and transfer it to the preheated oven.
Cook the stew in a bain-marie for 2 1/2 hours, or until the beef is tender and the vegetables are cooked through.
Remove the stew from the oven and stir in the chopped parsley.
Serve the stew hot, garnished with additional parsley if desired.
